name: Quick Validation
description: Validate problem, market, and monetization before building

steps.name: Problem Validation - Reddit Research
steps.prompt: Let's validate this problem is real. Search Reddit for people complaining about this problem. Look for subreddits where your target audience hangs out. Find at least 3 real posts/comments showing this pain.
instructions: Search relevant subreddits for keywords related to the problem
Look for complaint posts, rants, 'Is there a tool for...' questions
Capture direct quotes that show real pain
Document the subreddit names for later marketing

inputs.label: Reddit Evidence
inputs.placeholder: Paste links and quotes from Reddit posts...
inputs.helpText: At least 3 examples of people experiencing this problem

steps.name: Problem Validation - Twitter/X Research
steps.prompt: Now search Twitter/X for people asking for solutions or complaining about this problem. Look for 'Is there a tool that...', 'I wish there was...', or frustration tweets.
instructions: Search Twitter for problem-related keywords
Look for solution requests and complaints
Note influencers or accounts in this space
Capture direct quotes

inputs.label: Twitter/X Evidence
inputs.placeholder: Paste links and quotes from tweets...
inputs.helpText: Examples of people seeking solutions on Twitter

steps.name: Problem Validation - Severity Rating
steps.prompt: Based on your research, how severe is this problem? Rate it from 1 (Mild Annoyance) to 5 (Hair on Fire).
instructions: Consider: Does this problem cause money loss?
Consider: Is this a recurring problem or one-time?
Consider: How emotional are the complaints?
Be honest - don't inflate the rating

options.label: 5 - Hair on fire, will pay anything

scoring.description: Problem causes money loss or time waste

steps.name: Market Validation - Target Audience
steps.prompt: Who exactly is your target audience? Be specific - 'small business owners' is too broad. Think 'solo freelance web developers who work with 3-5 clients at a time'.
instructions: Be as specific as possible
One sentence that identifies WHO they are
Include context about their situation
This should match the people you found in research

inputs.label: Target Audience
inputs.placeholder: e.g., Solo freelance developers who struggle to manage client invoices...
inputs.helpText: One specific sentence describing your ideal customer

steps.name: Market Validation - Communities
steps.prompt: Where does your target audience hang out online? List at least 2 communities, forums, or platforms where you can reach them.
instructions: Include subreddits from earlier research
Look for Discord servers, Slack communities
Consider niche forums, Facebook groups
Think about where they spend time professionally

inputs.label: Communities
inputs.placeholder:
List communities with links...
- r/subreddit (link)
- Discord server name
- etc.
inputs.helpText: At least 2 places where you can reach your audience

scoring.description: You have access to this audience

steps.name: Market Validation - Size Estimate
steps.prompt: Rough estimate: how many people have this problem? Even a ballpark helps. Consider: subreddit sizes, community members, industry stats.
instructions: This doesn't need to be precise
Look at subreddit subscriber counts
Search for industry reports or stats
Even 'thousands' vs 'millions' is useful

inputs.label: How did you estimate this?
inputs.placeholder: Subreddit has X subscribers, industry report says Y...

steps.name: Monetization Validation - Competitor Research
steps.prompt: Find existing paid solutions (competitors). If people pay for alternatives, they'll pay for yours. Document at least 2 competitors with their pricing.
instructions: Search Google, Product Hunt, G2, Capterra
Note their pricing models (subscription, one-time, freemium)
Document what they do well and poorly
If NO competitors exist, ask why - usually a bad sign

inputs.label: Competitors
inputs.placeholder:
Competitor 1:
- URL: 
- Pricing: 
- Strengths: 
- Weaknesses: 

Competitor 2:
...
inputs.helpText: At least 2 competitors with pricing and analysis

steps.name: Monetization Validation - Gap Analysis
steps.prompt: What gap in the market can you exploit? How would your solution be different or better than what exists?
instructions: Identify a clear differentiator
Could be: simpler, cheaper, faster, better UX, specific niche
If you can't find a gap, reconsider the idea
The gap should be valuable to customers

inputs.label: Market Gap / Differentiation
inputs.placeholder: Our solution would be different because...

inputs.label: Estimated Price Point
inputs.helpText: Based on competitor pricing, what could you charge?

scoring.description: Gap in market you can exploit

steps.name: Calculate Total Score
steps.prompt: Let me calculate your validation score based on everything we've gathered...
instructions: Sum up all scoring criteria that were met
Maximum possible: 125 points
90+ = GREEN LIGHT (proceed)
60-89 = YELLOW (needs more research)
Below 60 = RED (kill or pivot)

steps.name: Make Decision
steps.prompt: Based on the validation score, here's the verdict. Score: {totalScore}/125
instructions: 90-125: GREEN LIGHT - Move to active, proceed to scope
60-89: YELLOW - Needs more research or pivoting
Below 60: RED - Kill it or move to killed folder

steps.name: Update Idea File
steps.prompt: I'll update your idea file with all the validation data and move it to the appropriate folder.
instructions: Add validation score and breakdown to idea file
Document key findings (quotes, links, competitor URLs)
If GREEN: move to _vault/active/
If RED: move to _vault/killed/ with reason

criteria.description: Problem causes money loss or time waste
You have access to this audience
Gap in market you can exploit

outputs.name: researchEvidence
outputs.description: All collected research links and quotes

qualityGates.description: Used the rubric, not gut feeling
qualityGates.errorMessage: Validation score must be calculated using the rubric
Must have documented evidence from research
Idea file must be updated with validation data

transition.message: Validation passed! Your idea scored {validationScore}/125. Ready to lock MVP scope.

pitfalls.fix: Actively search for reasons it WON'T work
If no competitors, ask why (usually bad sign)
Find REAL people, not imaginary ones
Have someone else review your scoring
Time-box to 4 hours max, then decide
